contacts.name: Seth Reynolds
contacts.title: Director of Sales
contacts.message: Doubling revenue from $800K to $1.6M while building a canvas team from scratch is hard. You know exactly how painful it is to find reps who can knock doors and close — that's the problem we built GojiBerry to solve.

contacts.name: Sandy Farrell
contacts.title: Sales & Marketing Director
contacts.message: Running both sales and marketing for a family roofing operation in Houston — that's a big plate. The Texas storm market creates demand spikes that are hard to staff for and then you're overstaffed in the off-season. We help sales directors solve exactly that cycle.

contacts.name: Jennifer Jernigan
contacts.title: Director of Business Development
contacts.message: Owens Corning certified in Nashville means volume commitments — which means you need consistent sales production, not just a good quarter. The rep hiring pressure in a market that hot is relentless. Happy to show you what we're doing for other crews like yours.

contacts.name: Dominic Swingle
contacts.title: Sales Director
contacts.message: Three generations in roofing and you're still out here grinding as Sales Director — that's not just a title, you actually care about the team performing. Family companies that are growing fast hit a wall when the old hiring playbook stops working. Happy to show you what's changed.

contacts.name: Steve Hadley
contacts.title: Sales Manager
contacts.message: San Antonio's been growing fast, which is great until you need to staff up quickly after a hail event. Sales manager in roofing knows that rep pipeline is always either empty or overflowing. Would love to connect on how we help teams like Elite stay ready.

contacts.name: Lewis Antonoff
contacts.title: Sales Manager
contacts.message: Running pest sales in the NY metro is a different beast — density is there but so is the competition for good reps. Sales manager with real P&L skin in the game is exactly who we built this for. Would love to connect.

contacts.name: Spenser Morgan
contacts.title: Founder & CEO
contacts.message: You've built an entire summer sales infrastructure around door-to-door pest — that's rare and impressive. But running that kind of seasonal volume means the pipeline and offboarding cycle never stops. Worth a quick chat about how we help founders like you smooth that out?

contacts.name: Todd Kiefer
contacts.company: Tiger Plumbing, Heating, Air Conditioning Services
contacts.title: General Manager

contacts.name: Charlie Conklin
contacts.company: Sentry Heating, Air Conditioning, Plumbing & Electrical
contacts.title: Owner
contacts.message: Birmingham's been growing steady and running HVAC, plumbing, and electrical under one roof means your sales team is always stretched. Owner-operator managing all three service lines knows the rep hiring problem better than anyone. Happy to connect.

contacts.name: Rick Rogers
contacts.company: Del-Air Heating, Air Conditioning, Plumbing and Electrical
contacts.title: Sales Leader
contacts.message: Del-Air's been one of the major multi-trade brands in DFW for years — that means sales complexity across HVAC, plumbing, and electrical. The rep hiring bar in the Dallas market is high and the competition is fierce. Happy to connect on what we're seeing.

contacts.name: Scott McLeod
contacts.company: Fields & Fowler HVAC, Plumbing, Electrical & Generators
contacts.title: Sales Director

contacts.name: David Massey
contacts.title: Founder & Owner
contacts.message: Roofing and solar in San Diego is about as premium a combo as it gets — but it also means you need reps who can sell a $30K+ job, which is a different animal than storm chasers. Would love to connect on what works for premium residential sales.

contacts.name: Douglas Ford
contacts.title: President
contacts.message: Tampa's roofing market has been insane since Ian — which is great until you're managing the feast-or-famine rep cycle that comes with it. You've been at this since 2009, so you've seen that swing before. Happy to show you what we're doing differently.

contacts.name: Gene Judd
contacts.title: Owner & President
contacts.message: Roofing Contractor of the Year with $183M and still posting sales rep roles — that tells me you've figured out growth but the rep pipeline never stops being a challenge. We work with residential contractors just like Bone Dry on exactly that problem.

contacts.name: Wiley Moss
contacts.title: Owner & Founder
contacts.message: Founded Moss Roofing in one of the most competitive markets in the country — you've had to figure out how to hire and retain reps in a market where everyone's poaching. That's a hard problem and it's the one we built GojiBerry to solve.

contacts.name: Jim Wobbles
contacts.title: Director of Sales
contacts.message: Fire protection outside sales is one of the hardest rep hires — the product knowledge bar is higher than roofing or pest but the territory model is the same. Director of Sales who's living that challenge is exactly who I built this for. Worth a quick chat.

contacts.name: Justin Koen
contacts.title: CEO
contacts.message: Running a landscaping company as CEO in Alabama with the outside sales model is a different beast from the service-only guys. Territory-based selling in lawn means rep turnover hits hard in the off-season. Would love to connect on how we address that.

contacts.name: Lavonne Dideon
contacts.title: President
contacts.message: Running a pest solutions company in the PNW as president — the spring season hire crunch has to be real every year. Would love to connect and show you what we're doing for other pest operators who are tired of rebuilding their sales team from scratch annually.

contacts.name: Erin Richardson
contacts.title: President
contacts.message: 63 years is a long time to earn a reputation — and now you're in your own words in high-growth mode. That's an interesting position: a legacy brand scaling like a startup. The rep hiring and retention piece is usually where that tension shows up most.

contacts.name: James McIntyre
contacts.title: President
contacts.message: Three new offices in a year across NJ, PA, and MD — that's aggressive expansion by any measure. With Anticimex behind you, the pressure to fill territories fast is real. We specialize in helping multi-location pest operators keep their rep pipelines full.

contacts.name: Christopher Helton
contacts.title: Co-Owner & VP
contacts.message: Asheville's been one of the fastest-growing markets in the Southeast and Gibson's right in it. Co-owner running sales knows the rep problem firsthand — not just as a line item but as a daily grind. Happy to show you what we do for pest companies your size.

contacts.name: Isaac Dixon
contacts.title: Founder & CEO
contacts.message: Former Sales Director who went and built his own pest company — you know exactly why it's hard to find reps who perform like you did. Bug Guard's future hiring is going to look a lot like your past experience. That's the gap we help founders fill.

contacts.name: Mark Hendricks
contacts.title: President
contacts.message: Pest control in Chicago is year-round and competitive — and president-level means you feel every open territory in the market. General Pest's been in it long enough to know that the rep pipeline problem doesn't get easier as you scale. Happy to connect.

contacts.name: Addi Aloya
contacts.title: President & CEO
contacts.message: Two acquisitions in 8 months is serious velocity. With 300+ in the field scaling across SW Florida, finding reps who actually stick has to be a constant headache. Would love to show you how we help PE-backed HVAC teams solve that.

contacts.name: Nicholas Steele
contacts.title: Sales Development Manager
contacts.message: Sales development in Atlanta roofing right now is as competitive as it gets. Building out a rep team in that market while storm season is on is a grind. Would love to show you what teams like Complete are doing to build a pipeline that doesn't dry up.

contacts.name: Corinne Ortiz
contacts.title: Vice President
contacts.message: Congrats on the 2024 40 Under 40 — Tucson's pest market is going to keep growing and the rep turnover challenge only gets harder as you scale. Would love to connect with someone who's clearly building something serious there.

contacts.name: Shane Oswalt
contacts.title: Vice President
contacts.message: Pest plus wildlife removal is a two-product D2D sell — which means you need reps with higher conviction than pure pest guys. VP at a company building that niche knows the rep profile is different. Would love to connect.

contacts.name: Randy Saxton
contacts.title: Regional VP
contacts.message: Saw Knox is actively hiring Sales Executives in Columbus — that search never really ends in pest, does it? Would love to show you how we help regional operators shorten that cycle and find reps who actually fit the territory model.

contacts.name: Shane Sawyer
contacts.title: VP of Sales and Operations
contacts.message: Running sales ops in Midland means dealing with energy-cycle workforce swings that most pest operators don't have to deal with. VP of both Sales and Ops is a brutal combo when rep turnover is constant. Happy to show you how we address that.

contacts.name: Devin Kleinpeter
contacts.title: Sales Manager
contacts.message: TOP TEAM Roofing is a name that signals you take sales culture seriously — that kind of branding attracts reps but keeping them is where most roofing companies fall short. Would love to connect and share what we're doing for D2D contractors.

contacts.name: Matthew Geyer
contacts.title: Sales Director
contacts.message: Utah home services is growing fast and you're running sales across HVAC, plumbing, and electrical under one brand. That's a tough act to scale — each trade has different rep DNA. Would love to show you how we help multi-trade sales directors build pipelines for all three.

contacts.name: Brian Vardiman
contacts.company: Service Lion Plumbing Heating Air Electric
contacts.title: President
contacts.message: Service Lion — that's a strong brand for a multi-trade home services company. President running plumbing, heating, air, and electric means the sales team spans multiple service lines. The rep complexity there is real. Happy to connect.

contacts.name: Sammy Shelton
contacts.company: Four Seasons Air Conditioning Heating & Refrigeration
contacts.title: Owner

contacts.name: Mike Myers
contacts.title: VP Sales
contacts.message: Abacus just picked up multiple awards in Houston and Austin in 2025 — that kind of momentum means the rep hiring bar just went up. VP Sales at a multi-trade brand that's actively scaling is exactly the conversation I want to be having. Would love to connect.

contacts.name: Chelsea Stone
contacts.title: Regional VP of Sales
contacts.message: Regional VP in Tampa covering home services territory — that means you're constantly evaluating rep performance across a big footprint. The hardest part of that role is knowing which markets are understaffed before revenue drops. Happy to connect.

contacts.name: Jeff Stamps
contacts.title: Sales Director
contacts.message: Multi-trade companies like Lee's — plumbing, HVAC, roofing all under one roof — have a sales complexity that single-vertical guys don't. You're essentially managing four different rep profiles at once. That's the problem we love to work on.

contacts.name: Kevin Citarella
contacts.title: Owner
contacts.message: Florida pest control is as close to a guaranteed market as it gets — the problem is everyone knows it, so finding and keeping good reps is brutal. Owner of a growing FL pest company knows that cycle better than most. Would love to connect.

contacts.name: James Diffenderfer
contacts.title: Sales Director
contacts.message: You're developing future leaders at TEC while running sales — that's a mentoring play that works until you're the bottleneck. Exterior contractor with outside sales model in Lancaster is exactly the profile we built GojiBerry for. Would love to connect.

contacts.name: Carter Gregory
contacts.title: Sales Manager
contacts.message: Nashville roofing market is as good as it gets right now and you're clearly grinding in D2D. Sales managers who have actually knocked doors themselves are rare and they feel the rep quality problem more than anyone. Would love to connect.
